perturbation.original_text: Subsequent contributions will be provided by Kiromic to the common fund upon presentation of individual purchase orders and/or proofs of expenditure —which will be paid for each time starting upon the successful IPO (Initial Public Offering) of the Kiromic's common shares.
perturbation.changed_text: Subsequent contributions will be provided by Kiromic to the common fund upon presentation of individual purchase orders and/or proofs of expenditure —which will be paid for each time within 30 days of presentation, regardless of the IPO status.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the condition for payment. Originally, Kiromic's contributions were contingent upon a successful IPO. Now, payment is due within 30 days of presenting purchase orders, irrespective of whether the IPO occurred. This contradicts the original financial commitment structure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subsequent contributions will be provided by Kiromic to the common fund upon presentation of individual purchase orders and/or proofs of expenditure —which will be paid for each time. Molipharma undertakes to financially support the entire research program against sars-cov-2. By way of example, Molipharma undertakes to finance the following items, either directly or indirectly through research grants or other non-diluting funds, awarded by European and/or Italian Institutions: a. The expenses for the supply of equipment and materials, as well as those related to their ordinary and extraordinary maintenance, necessary for the development of the program; b. Medical and subsistence expenses in favour of the patients who will be selected for the clinical trials and any expenses necessary for third party vendors (such as Contract Research Organizations, central labs, couriers, etc…) necessary for planning and executing such clinical trials; c. Funding of scholarships and/or research grants for the staff who will be assigned to the research and development of the projects referred to in point 2; d. Funding of educational or training initiatives. Subsequent contributions will be provided by Molipharma to the common fund upon presentation of individual purchase orders and/or proofs of expenditure - which will be paid for each time.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Kiromic's contributions were contingent upon a successful IPO, whereas the changed text mandates payment within 30 days of presentation, irrespective of the IPO status. This directly contradicts the conditionality implied in the contradicted text, which also discusses Kiromic's contributions, creating an inconsistency regarding the payment terms for those contributions. Therefore, it creates a direct and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Steering Committee shall appoint, within 30 days of the signature of this JV, a Technical Committee composed of one representative of each of the Parties in relation to each specific -3- clinical trial program, which shall have the function of coordinating the technical and administrative activities to be undertaken in the framework of this JV.
perturbation.changed_text: The Steering Committee may appoint, within 60 days of the signature of this JV, a Technical Committee composed of one representative of Kiromic in relation to each specific -3- clinical trial program, but Molipharma is only required to have representatives attend on an 'as needed' basis, which shall have the function of coordinating the technical and administrative activities to be undertaken in the framework of this JV.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction concerning the composition and mandatory nature of the Technical Committee. The original text mandates the appointment of a Technical Committee composed of representatives from *both* parties within 30 days. The altered text changes the timeframe to 60 days, makes Molipharma's representation optional ('as needed' basis), and only mandates Kiromic to have representatives. This introduces inconsistency regarding the structure and responsibilities of the committee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Steering Committee shall appoint, within 30 days of the signature of this JV, a Technical Committee composed of one representative of each of the Parties in relation to each specific -3- clinical trial program, which shall have the function of coordinating the technical and administrative activities to be undertaken in the framework of this JV. The tasks assigned to the Technical Committee are to: a. propose any new project to be developed to the Steering Committee; b. define the specific guidelines for each project and check the execution processes and timelines implemented under this JV; c. check at least quarterly the progress of the clinical development programs, the correct implementation of the commitments undertaken, including the economic ones; in the event of failure by one of the Parties to comply with these commitments, the Technical Committee shall promptly inform the Steering Committee; d. report, every six months, to the Steering Committee on the activities carried out and the results achieved under the Agreement; e. propose to the Steering Committee any changes in the projects referred to in point 2 and/or any changes in the economic commitments made and their utilization.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the composition and mandatory nature of the Technical Committee. The original and contradicted text mandates the appointment of a Technical Committee composed of representatives from *both* parties within 30 days. The altered text changes the timeframe to 60 days, makes Molipharma's representation optional ('as needed' basis), and only mandates Kiromic to have representatives, creating an inconsistency as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. This violates the original and contradicted text's expectation of both parties having representatives.

perturbation.original_text: The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days.
perturbation.changed_text: The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days, unless Kiromic exercises the withdrawal, in which case, no notice is required.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the notice period required for withdrawal. Originally, a minimum of 30 days' notice was required for *any* withdrawal. The modified text creates an exception where Kiromic can withdraw without any notice, contradicting the initial requirement and favoring one party over the other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may withdraw from this JV only for serious and justified reasons or by mutual consent. The withdrawal must be exercised by written notice, to be sent to the other Party by certified letter or PEC, with minimum notice of 30 days. Withdrawal or termination by mutual consent shall only have effect for the future and shall not affect the part of the Agreement already executed. In case of withdrawal according to the previous paragraph, Kiromic is obliged to cover Molipharma for the expenses incurred and for those committed, related to the research programs being developed, until receipt of the notice of withdrawal.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that a minimum of 30 days' notice is required for withdrawal. The changed text introduces an exception for Kiromic, allowing them to withdraw without any notice. This directly contradicts the previously stated 30-day notice requirement, creating an inconsistency as described in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
